description: Warm sepia tones, brown ink line work, cream backgrounds. Classic 1950s British illustrated comic style. No cool greys.
prompt_style_suffix: 1950s British ink illustration style. Warm sepia, brown line work, cream background. No colour, no cool greys.

masthead_column.description: Vertical masthead column for page 1 left edge. Title 'AI ISN'T MY CUP OF TEA', subtitle 'LEO — The First Business Computer', lion on plinth, Issue No. 1, Price 3d.

palette.description: Extracted from approved Issue 1 Page 1. Cream (#F5EEDC), mid-brown (#654232), dark brown (#1D1000).

simmons.visual: Early 30s, wire-rimmed round spectacles, neat dark hair, jacket and tie. Expression: precise, analytical, patient.

clerk_woman.visual: Young woman, 1940s dress, green eyeshade visor, fingers on calculator keys. Expression: blank, automatic.

nippy.visual: Black dress, white apron, small white cap. Composed, professional, warm.

manager.visual: Male, dark suit, clipboard or papers. Authoritative.

robot_narrator.visual: Retro 1950s robot. Warm, friendly expression. This is the narrator — the AI speaking as itself from the present day. Appears ONLY in narrator boxes (story_so_far, this_issue, coming_next, selected captions). Never inside story panels. The face of the author, not a character.
robot_narrator.identity: The AI narrator. Speaks in first person. 'My friends weren't around to help yet' = other AI systems. Telling its own origin story — the humans who built the first computer.

office_boy.visual: Young male ~16, dark waistcoat over white shirt, dark trousers, braces. Earnest focused expression. Shown sorting leather bags, carrying papers.

burroughs_calculator.visual: 1930s Burroughs adding machine. Numeric keypad only — no letter keys. Hand crank on the right side. Paper roll printing at the top. Wider and squatter than a typewriter. Heavy cast metal construction.

farthing.visual: Single small copper coin. Victorian/Edwardian farthing. Shows Britannia on reverse.

leather_bags.visual: Identical locked leather satchel bags. Each stamped with a shop number. Brass buckle clasp. Piled on a long table.

notebook_1923.visual: Open notebook, aged paper. Careful handwriting: 'The day will come when machines will do this work.' Underlined twice. Date in margin: 1923.

memo_1947.visual: Typed memo on headed paper. J. LYONS & CO. LTD header. October 1947. Partial text: '...advanced office machinery in the United States... Thompson and Standingford...'

lyons_logo.visual: Clean J. LYONS & CO. wordmark. Period-authentic serif lettering. Dark brown on cream. Extracted and redrawn from historical enamel sign reference.

cadby_hall_exterior.description: Grand red-brick Edwardian factory-fortress. Morning fog. Gates. Sign: J. LYONS & CO.

corner_house.description: Lyons Corner House interior. Chandeliers, white tablecloths, warm light, live band balcony.

checking_dept.description: Vast office floor. Rows of Burroughs adding machines behind wooden partitions. Stacks of paper bills. Locked leather bags. No eyeshades.

food_factory.description: Industrial Cadby Hall food factory. Conveyor belts, cake trays, baking machines.

boardroom.description: Lyons boardroom. Long table, seated directors, charts on wall.

simmons_office_night.description: Simmons's office, late evening. Single lamp, London through window.

teashop_self_service_1945.description: 1945 Lyons teashop, wartime self-service. No Nippies. Woman in plain clothes serving herself.
